title: Pham Thi Truc Phuong
short_description:
Pham Thi Truc Phuong, 14 years old, currently a student at Nguyen Van Cu Secondary School (Bac Ninh Hoa commune, Khanh Hoa province). Phuong's childhood continuously experienced great losses when she lost her father and older brother, respectively - two men who were the family's support.
In 2020, Phuong's father passed away due to an accident at work while working away from home. That pain still hasn't subsided. In June 2025, the family continued to receive shock when my older brother unfortunately passed away in a traffic accident. Two consecutive departures put the family's life in an unstable situation both mentally and economically.
Phuong currently lives with her mother, her sister Nguyen Thi Dung (39 years old), grandmother Tran Thi Noi (78 years old) and younger brother Pham Gia Huy (11 years old), 5th grade at Nguyen Luu Primary School.
story:
Pham Thi Truc Phuong, 14 years old, currently a student at Nguyen Van Cu Secondary School (Bac Ninh Hoa commune, Khanh Hoa province). Phuong's childhood continuously experienced great losses when she lost her father and older brother, respectively - two men who were the family's support.
In 2020, Phuong's father passed away due to an accident at work while working away from home. That pain still hasn't subsided. In June 2025, the family continued to receive shock when my older brother unfortunately passed away in a traffic accident. Two consecutive departures put the family's life in an unstable situation both mentally and economically.
Phuong currently lives with her mother, her sister Nguyen Thi Dung (39 years old), grandmother Tran Thi Noi (78 years old) and younger brother Pham Gia Huy (11 years old), 5th grade at Nguyen Luu Primary School.
After the events, Phuong's mother became the sole breadwinner of the family. She makes a living by selling grilled spring rolls on the sidewalk. Every day from 2:00 p.m. to 9:00 p.m., she sets up temporary tables and chairs in front of her neighbor's house to sell. The average income is only about 100,000–150,000 VND/day and completely depends on the weather. When the rainy season comes, many days of not being able to do business means the whole family has almost no income.
Difficulties were compounded when Ms. Dung had an eye problem. One eye has completely lost sight since she was 5 years old, and the other eye is also weakening over time. Even so, she still tries to work every day to support her elderly mother and two small children.
Phuong's grandmother is old, often sick and can only help with a few small tasks around the house. Every month she receives an allowance of 500,000 VND. Meanwhile, the family has to bear many fixed expenses such as electricity bill of about 500,000 VND and water bill of about 350,000 VND per month. Not having enough resources, Phuong's mother often had to borrow more and currently still owes about 5 million VND for raw materials for sales and market fees.
Loving her mother's hard work, after school, Phuong always helps her mother prepare ingredients, sell goods, and take care of her grandmother. I also proactively do housework to help my mother feel less stressed.
When talking about her father and brother, Phuong still couldn't hold back her tears. I remember your promise that when you have money when you go to work, you will buy me new clothes. But that promise can never be fulfilled.
After all the losses, Phuong only loved her mother more. I wish one day I could buy my mother a new set of clothes, because she hasn't thought about herself for so long. Phuong always tries her best to study with the hope of becoming a solid support to take care of her mother, grandmother and younger brother.

title: Tran Thi Hong Tham
short_description:
Tran Thi Hong Tham, 18 years old, currently a student at To Van On High School, living with his grandfather in Tu Bong commune, Khanh Hoa province. Tham's childhood was filled with many losses when her parents divorced when she was young, and then both passed away due to illness. From then on, Tham grew up under the care of his grandfather and the love of his aunt.
My grandfather is Mr Tran Ngoc Son, is 86 years old this year. In his late years, he was still the only relative staying by Tham's side every day, but his health became increasingly weak.
story:
Tran Thi Hong Tham, 18 years old, currently a student at To Van On High School, living with his grandfather in Tu Bong commune, Khanh Hoa province. Tham's childhood was filled with many losses when her parents divorced when she was young, and then both passed away due to illness. From then on, Tham grew up under the care of his grandfather and the love of his aunt.
My grandfather is Mr Tran Ngoc Son, is 86 years old this year. In his late years, he was still the only relative staying by Tham's side every day, but his health became increasingly weak. Every month, he has to go to the medical station for examination and to get blood pressure medicine. In the house that was built many decades ago, the two grandchildren relied on each other amid the lack of success. The small house has seriously degraded, many parts leak when it rains. The living space is so narrow that sleeping, studying and cooking are all in the same place. Even so, Tham always tries to arrange everything neat and tidy. I also save small amounts of money to buy simple decorations, just hoping that my house will become more cozy.
Besides his grandfather, Tham's biggest supporter is his auntTran Thi My Lien, 51 years old. She has her own family and three children, but still takes on the lives of the Tham sisters. Every day, she wakes up very early to prepare to sell banh canh at the market. When the sale was finished, she hurriedly ran to Tham's house to take care of her grandfather and her two sisters. Her life always revolves between two families, but she never complains.
Tham's younger brother is Tran Gia Bao, 10 years old, currently studying in grade 4 at Van Phuoc Primary School. Since birth, Bao has had a brain hemorrhage, is often sick and has to go in and out of the hospital. Poor health and mental problems prevent her from taking care of herself like other normal children. Because he was raised by Aunt Lien since childhood, Bao always thought she was his biological mother. Although she loves her very much, Tham still cannot take her to live with her because her situation is too lonely, which is also the thing that torments her the most.
Each month, the Tham sisters receive an orphan allowance of 1,500,000 VND. That money was sent to my aunt to cover food for both sisters and grandfather. The only gift his father left for Tham was an old bicycle, but it is now damaged and no longer usable.
After school, Tham is always busy returning home to cook, clean and take care of his grandfather. Even though life is lacking in many ways, I still keep beautiful dreams in my heart. I sincerely hope that in the future I can become an athlete and earn a lot of money to become a support for my grandfather, younger brother and aunt who sacrificed so much for their family.
What Tham longs for most is not for herself, but one day she can live near her younger brother, so that the two sisters no longer have to be apart amid so many losses in life.

title: Nguyen Cam Uyen
short_description:
Nguyen Cam Uyen, 18 years old, currently a student at Nguyen Du High School No. 2, living in Uar commune, Gia Lai province. At an age when many of her friends are still protected by their parents, Uyen soon learned how to grow up amid the constant losses of life.
In 2009, when Uyen was just 1 year old, her father passed away after a work accident, leaving her mother alone to raise three young daughters, Thanh Tam, Thanh Mai and Cam Uyen.
story:
Nguyen Cam Uyen, 18 years old, currently a student at Nguyen Du High School No. 2, living in Uar commune, Gia Lai province. At an age when many of her friends are still protected by their parents, Uyen soon learned how to grow up amid the constant losses of life.
In 2009, when Uyen was just 1 year old, her father passed away after a work accident, leaving her mother alone to raise three young daughters, Thanh Tam, Thanh Mai and Cam Uyen. It was thought that after her mother took another step in 2013, the family would have more support when her youngest sister Hieu Thao was born, but incidents continued to happen. When Thao was only 6 years old, her stepfather left the family and passed away a few years later.
The biggest tragedy came in 2015 when Uyen's mother was diagnosed with leukemia. During the next 8 years, the four sisters' childhood was associated with hospitals, prescriptions and the journey with their mother to fight the disease. The children took turns taking care of and encouraging their mother in the hope of keeping her with the family longer. But on November 16, 2023, mother still could not overcome the evil disease, leaving four sisters alone in life.
Since the day my mother passed away, my eldest sister Nguyen Huu Thanh Tam put aside his college dream to go to Ho Chi Minh City to work as a worker. Every month, she saves about 2 million VND to take care of her children's education and pay interest on the 30 million VND debt the family borrowed to treat her mother's illness. Second sister
Nguyen Thi Thanh Mai. Now that I have my own family, life is also very difficult, so I can only support my children when I have the conditions.
Not wanting to be a burden to her sisters, Uyen took advantage of everything outside of school hours to help support her family. I used to work at a coffee shop, type documents for hire, and even hire anyone to work in the fields and I was willing to do it. Even though her life is lacking in many ways, Uyen has always maintained her achievements as a good student for many years in a row.
What torments Uyen the most is not her own hardship, but the image of her eldest sister who spent most of her youth sacrificing for her younger siblings. I always hope to study well so I can enter university, pursue my dream of becoming a teacher, and find a stable job to take care of my youngest sister Hieu Thao and so Thanh Tam can live for herself after years of sacrificing so much.
Having lost her father at a young age, and then losing her mother after a long journey fighting illness, Uyen still chose to move forward strongly. For me, trying to study well is the way to respond to my mother's love and my sisters' silent sacrifices.

title: Le Anh Ngoc Diem
short_description: Le Anh Ngoc Diem, 17 years old, is currently a student at High School No. 1 in Ea Drang commune, Dak Lak province. I grew up in a small family including my parents and two younger sisters, Le Thi Thao Nguyen (12 years old) and Le Bao Thi (10 years old). Once having a simple but loving life, Diem's ​​family fell into crisis when her father fell seriously ill.
story:
Le Anh Ngoc Diem, 17 years old, is currently a student at High School No. 1 in Ea Drang commune, Dak Lak province. I grew up in a small family including my parents and two younger sisters, Le Thi Thao Nguyen (12 years old) and Le Bao Thi (10 years old). Once having a simple but loving life, Diem's ​​family fell into crisis when her father fell seriously ill.
Diem's ​​father - Mr. Le Xuan Dinh has Parkinson's disease with severe movement disorders. The disease causes his body to frequently tremble, stiffen, lose balance, and make walking and daily activities extremely difficult. In 2018, when Diem's ​​mother, Le Thi Thuy (37 years old), was diagnosed with the disease, she had to sell 7 acres of land - the family's largest asset - to treat her husband.
But after everything, Mr. Dinh's illness did not improve but became increasingly worse. Each month, his medicine costs range from 1.5 to 2 million VND. There were months when he didn't have enough money to buy medicine, so he only took it for about 20 days and then had to endure the pain in silence.
With no more productive land, the family is considered poor. All economic burdens now fall on Diem's ​​mother's shoulders. Ms. Thuy does everything to make a living: during the day she works weed, picks coffee, picks pepper with an income of about 200-250 thousand VND/day. At night, if there is a wedding or death anniversary in the neighborhood, she takes the opportunity to wash dishes for a fee of 25,000 VND per hour.
Working hard from morning to night made her health increasingly depleted, her body often ached, but she never dared to go to the doctor. She was afraid that if she fell, her family would have no one to support her.
Amidst these deprivations, Ngoc Diem is still an obedient and energetic girl. Outside of school, I help my mother clean the house, cook, take care of pigs, and tutor my two younger siblings in studying. On holidays, I also work with my mother to share some of the burden.
Seeing her father in pain every day, Diem always felt helpless. I can't do anything great except try to study well, live well and become a spiritual support for my family. I have a dream of becoming a fashion designer in the future not only to pursue my passion, but also to help my parents and two younger siblings have a better life.
For Diem, happiness right now is simply that her father is a little less hurt, her mother is a little less miserable, and the family is still together.

title: Le Thi Hong Nhu
short_description: Le Thi Hong Nhu, 18 years old, is currently a student at Nguyen Van Linh High School (Hoa Hiep ward, Dak Lak province). At an age when she should only focus on studying and youthful dreams, Nhu soon had to become a spiritual support for the whole family after consecutive incidents.
story:
Le Thi Hong Nhu, 18 years old, is currently a student at Nguyen Van Linh High School (Hoa Hiep ward, Dak Lak province). At an age when she should only focus on studying and youthful dreams, Nhu soon had to become a spiritual support for the whole family after consecutive incidents.
Nhu's mother passed away due to underlying illness and Covid-19 infection. Before the pain could subside, in May 2025, her father also passed away, leaving Nhu without both parents. Since then, I have lived with my grandmother Tran Thi But (78 years old), my uncle Le Van Tru (36 years old) and my cousin Le Thi Truc, who is only 10 months old.
The events did not stop there. Mr. Tru's wife left when baby Truc was only 20 days old. Since then, my grandmother is old and weak and has to take care of her grandchildren. She has almost no eyesight and has many diseases in her body. Meanwhile, Uncle Tru works as a seafarer, his job is unstable, sometimes he goes for months before returning and only leaves a small amount of money for his family's living expenses.
Because of economic difficulties, families often fall into poverty. There were times when baby Truc didn't have any milk to drink, so the whole family had to ask for milk or buy cheap milk to last. Even though she is 10 months old, the baby is still skinny and sick, making Nhu feel sad.
Currently, every month, Nhu receives an orphan allowance of 1,500,000 VND. I spent that money mainly on studying and traveling. The whole family's electricity and water bill is about 200,000 VND per month, and the internet for studying is also shared by neighbors.
When he goes to sea for a long time, Nhu becomes the one who takes care of everything in the family. She helps her take care of the baby, takes care of the housework and shares all the burdens within her ability. However, I still maintain a remarkable academic record as a good student for 12 consecutive years.
Her family is poor, but Nhu never thought about giving up her studies. I understand that only knowledge can help me change the future and protect my remaining loved ones.
What makes others admire Nhu is not only her strength but also her deep gratitude. I always keep in mind that in the future, if I have a stable job, I will take care of my grandmother and uncle and continue to help other difficult situations the same way I was loved and protected during the most lonely days of my life.

title: Luong Tan Dat
short_description: Luong Tan Dat, 15 years old, is currently a student at Nguyen Anh Hao Secondary School, living in Tay Hoa commune, Dak Lak province. From a young age, Dat grew up without his father's love and never had a fulfilling childhood like his peers.
story:
Luong Tan Dat, 15 years old, is currently a student at Nguyen Anh Hao Secondary School, living in Tay Hoa commune, Dak Lak province. From a young age, Dat grew up without his father's love and never had a fulfilling childhood like his peers.
My mother - Ms. Luong Thi Nhung (42 years old) has suffered from severe depression for the past 15 years. When she gets sick, she often screams, becomes disruptive, and has behavior that endangers herself and those around her. Therefore, the family had to arrange for her to live in a small house next to her grandmother's house for convenient care. Previously, she was taken to a mental hospital for treatment, but after Dat's grandfather passed away, the family could no longer pay the hospital fees so they had to take her home.
From the day he was born, Dat received almost no full care from his mother. The person who raised me all these years is my grandmother Le Thi Suong (75 years old). Despite her advanced age, weak health, and many illnesses, she still became the family's biggest supporter.
The family's main source of income is based on meager subsidies: Dat's mother receives 500,000 VND per month, and Dat receives nearly 700,000 VND. In addition, I also receive 500,000 VND every 3 months from the school and sponsors. To have more money to make ends meet, her grandmother grows vegetables around the house to sell, but some days she only earns about 30,000 VND. The family also has a small rice field. Thanks to relatives to help with farming, the amount of rice harvested is only enough to last the day.
Difficult circumstances also caused Dat's older brother - Luong Huynh Tan Thanh (17 years old) - to leave school early to work as a helper at a restaurant in Nha Trang. My income is not stable so I can only occasionally send her a few hundred thousand dong.
Grandma's house was built in 1983 and is now seriously degraded. During the big flood in November 2025, the water rose up to the roof of the house, the whole family had to break the roof tiles to climb up to avoid the flood in an extremely dangerous situation.
Feeling sorry for his grandmother who was getting older and weaker, Dat soon realized his responsibility. On weekends, I work as a weeder for people in the neighborhood, getting paid about 100,000 VND per day. I give all the money I earn to my grandmother to take care of meals for my family.
Even though he never fully felt motherly love, Dat never blamed his mother. I understand that my mother is the one who suffers the most. What I always wish for is to grow up quickly, have a stable job to take care of my mother, take care of my grandmother and make up for some of the sacrifices she has made for me over the years.
