title: Nguyen Thao Van
short_description: Nguyen Thao Van, 14 years old, is currently a student at Suoi Bac Secondary School (Son Hoa commune, Dak Lak province). Since childhood, Van did not have a father, her mother left when she was only 15 months old. My childhood was tied to only one person - my grandmother Nguyen Thi Hong (68 years old), who raised me for both my parents.
story:
Nguyen Thao Van, 14 years old, is currently a student at Suoi Bac Secondary School (Son Hoa commune, Dak Lak province). Since childhood, Van did not have a father, her mother left when she was only 15 months old. My childhood was tied to only one person - my grandmother Nguyen Thi Hong (68 years old), who raised me for both my parents.
My grandmother worked as an agricultural laborer. Everyone hired her to do whatever she wanted: pulling cassava, cutting grass, making glue, harvesting crops... Hard work, unstable income, but still the only source of living to feed her grandchildren. On days when she doesn't have work, she has to debit money for food and room, then wait until she has money to pay in installments.
Her house was in a remote area, more than 10km from school, and there was no means of transportation, so she had to send Van to stay near the school. From then on, at the age of 14, I had to live alone in a small rented room, taking care of everything myself: from meals, laundry to studying. Every month, she tries to send me about 1,000,000 VND to cover accommodation and food - a very small amount of money for life away from home.
There were times when she didn't have enough money, so Van had to eat less and eat on credit at Mrs. Dieu's house, waiting until her grandmother had money to pay. Meager meals and days of deprivation have become familiar. But what makes me saddest is not hunger, but the feeling of loneliness.
On nights alone in her rented room, Van often cried quietly because she missed her grandmother, missed her family, and longed to live in a loving embrace like many friends her age. On weekends, if you have time, I will be picked up to visit my grandmother - rare moments for me to feel the warmth of family.
However, Van never blamed her mother. I understand that my mother is mentally unstable and cannot control her behavior. In my heart, there is still only longing and a very simple wish: one day to see my mother again in a more peaceful state.
Currently, her grandmother's health is getting weaker after eye and knee surgery. That made Van always worry - when she was not yet grown up and had no time to help, she had to bear too much.
Amidst both material and emotional deprivation, Van still persevered in going to school and became independent every day. My dream is not big: I just hope to have a stable job in the future, so I can take care of my grandmother, live close to her, and no longer have to leave my family for the remaining years of my childhood.

title: Tran Khai Minh
short_description: Tran Khai Minh, 14 years old, is currently studying at Tran Cao Van Secondary School (Binh Kien ward, Dak Lak province). Minh's childhood was a series of days living far away from his family, without his father, his mother was seriously ill, and he had to rely on someone else's house to continue going to school.
story:
Tran Khai Minh, 14 years old, is currently studying at Tran Cao Van Secondary School (Binh Kien ward, Dak Lak province). Minh's childhood was a series of days living far away from his family, without his father, his mother was seriously ill, and he had to rely on someone else's house to continue going to school.
Minh's mother - Ms. Tran Thi Phuong (35 years old) has lupus erythematosus, a dangerous autoimmune disease that causes her health to become increasingly depleted. For nearly 10 years, she had to be treated at Cho Ray Hospital and was never able to return to visit her children. Minh's grandmother - Ms. Tran Thi Nghia (60 years old), even though she also has goiter, still has to stay in Ho Chi Minh City to take care of her daughter, and also sells lottery tickets every day with her son who is disabled in both legs to make money. From that small amount of money, she scrimped to send it back to her hometown for Minh to live on.
In the countryside, Minh's family's situation was no better. My grandfather is old, works as a seafarer, and sometimes only comes home once every few months. Minh's two other uncles are being treated for tuberculosis at Pham Ngoc Thach Hospital, all costs are weighing heavily on his shoulders.
Therefore, Minh could not live with his family but was taken in and raised by Ms. Nguyen Thi Hien - a neighbor and head of the Ward Women's Union. There was no biological relationship, but Ms. Hien extended a supportive hand, gave me a stable place to live and continued going to school. Minh lived in a small attic, simple but warm and human. For me, it is a great luck.
Minh is an understanding and affectionate boy. Living at Ms. Hien's house, she always proactively helps with housework, living obediently and gratefully. There were times when Minh saw her grandmother and family struggling so hard and thought about quitting school to work to help. But it was Ms. Hien who was always there to encourage me and keep me on the path of study.
Currently, each month, Minh receives about 700,000 VND in allowance from his mother's illness policy. This money was sent by my grandfather to support my living expenses.
Even though he lives far away from his mother and always carries the fear that one day he won't be able to see her again, Minh still keeps trying. I have a very clear dream: to become a lawyer, to be able to help weak people and difficult situations like my own family - so they have the opportunity to live a fairer and better life.

title: Ta Quang Phu
short_description: Ta Quang Phu, 13 years old, is currently a student at Nguyen Trung Truc Secondary School. His childhood was associated with the lack of love from parents. His parents divorced when he was very young, his mother went to work far away, his father had a new family, both of them no longer cared about his life. Since then, Phu has lived with his maternal great-grandparents - Mr. Huynh Van Tam (77 years old) and Mrs. Huynh Thi Thuy Lieu (74 years old), in extremely difficult circumstances.
story:
Ta Quang Phu, 13 years old, is currently a student at Nguyen Trung Truc Secondary School. His childhood was associated with the lack of love from parents. His parents divorced when he was very young, his mother went to work far away, his father had a new family, both of them no longer cared about his life. Since then, Phu has lived with his maternal great-grandparents - Mr. Huynh Van Tam (77 years old) and Mrs. Huynh Thi Thuy Lieu (74 years old), in extremely difficult circumstances.
The family is poor and does not have a stable source of income. The two grandparents are old, in poor health, no longer able to work, and only rely on an allowance of about 1,500,000 VND per month to take care of all three of them. My great-grandfather's eyes were blurred, his legs were weak, and he had difficulty walking; My great-grandmother was also often sick because of her old age. Daily activities often require the support of my aunt - my grandparents' eldest daughter, but that help is only temporary.
The small house where the three grandchildren live was built more than 20 years ago, located near the highway area. Even though we received help to repair the foundation, the roof still deteriorates and leaks every time it rains. There are only a few chickens raised around the house to improve meals, and life is lacking in everything.
Even though he did not receive full love from his parents, Phu did not blame him. I still love my mother and hope she has her own happiness. For me, being cared for and raised by my great-grandparents is a blessing. I grew up in the silent love of my grandparents - who are both father and mother.
What worries Phu the most is not the lack of material things, but the worry that his grandparents are getting old and weak. I'm afraid that one day, when I'm not yet grown up, my grandparents will no longer be by my side. There are times when I have low self-esteem and think that I am a burden, and even wonder if it is because I am not good enough that my parents left me.
However, beyond everything, Phu still kept a very clear dream: to continue going to school, have a stable job in the future to take care of his great-grandparents. For me, happiness is not a big thing, but simply that my grandparents are always healthy and can stay with me for a long time so that I can show filial piety and repay them for their upbringing.

title: Tran Thanh Thuy
short_description: Tran Thanh Thuy, 11 years old, is currently studying at Phong Du Primary and Secondary School, Tien Yen commune, Quang Ninh province. Thuy's childhood was tied to a small house that lacked the warmth of her mother, but was full of love from her father.
story:
Tran Thanh Thuy, 11 years old, is currently studying at Phong Du Primary and Secondary School, Tien Yen commune, Quang Ninh province. Thuy's childhood was tied to a small house that lacked the warmth of her mother, but was full of love from her father.
In 2019, their mother passed away from lung disease. At that time, the twin sisters were too young to fully understand the loss. Since the day his mother passed away, his father - Mr. Tran Van Hung (43 years old) alone shouldered the role of both father and mother, raising three children in extremely poor conditions.
To take care of his children, Mr. Hung did all kinds of work in the countryside: farming, growing acacia, peeling acacia for hire, loading and unloading... whoever hired him did whatever he wanted. The work is hard but the income is unstable, only enough to cover the minimum expenses for four children. He himself also has stomach disease and bone and joint pain, but still tries to work every day. He does not dare to work far away because he wants to stay close to take care of his children.
The family has a small field and produces two crops each year. After deducting expenses, the amount earned is not much, most of the rice is kept as food for the whole year. Life goes on day by day.
Thuy's older brother - Tran Duc Khiem (17 years old) is currently in 11th grade. Khiem is his mother's stepchild from her previous husband, but Hung always loves him like his own son. There has never been any discrimination, he still cares and worries for Khiem like two children. Khiem once expressed his desire to go to college, but worried that his father wouldn't be able to afford it. Those words made the father silent, his eyes were red because he loved his son, but he was helpless.
The house of four father and son was built before their mother passed away, but only the rough parts were completed. There are almost no valuable items inside, and living conditions are still lacking. Mr. Khiem does not have his own car, so he often has to help his friends get to school. The twin sisters ride each other every day on an old bicycle that has worn out over the years.
Understanding their father's hardships, all three brothers were very obedient. After school, the children help their father cook, clean the house, and share small household chores. Even though they are deprived, the children still grow up with the love and support of their father - who has devoted his whole life to filling the void left by his mother.
In that simple house, material things may not be enough, but family affection is always warm. The three children grew up with their father's silent sacrifice, and that love became the fulcrum that helped them continue steadily on the road ahead.

title: Ngo Minh Duong
short_description: Ngo Minh Duong, 12 years old, is studying at Yen Luong Secondary School - Van Thang commune, Ninh Binh province. Duong's childhood was associated with the days without his father and constant worry about his mother's health.
story:
Ngo Minh Duong, 12 years old, is studying at Yen Luong Secondary School - Van Thang commune, Ninh Binh province. Duong's childhood was associated with the days without his father and constant worry about his mother's health.
In 2021, my father - Mr. Ngo Van Vien passed away from an accident. That sudden death caused the family to lose their economic breadwinner, leaving the four mother and children struggling to manage life. My mother, Ms. Do Thi Son (39 years old), although depressed, still tried to stand up and became the only support for her three sisters.
The family's livelihood mainly depends on the fields. Ms. Son not only works on her own farm but also takes on other people's fields to farm. At the end of the crop, she returns the rice to the field owner, and stores the rest as food for the whole year. In her free time, she works as a mason, works in the fields, or goes to her brother's carpentry workshop to sand wood, as long as she has more income to support her children.
Working too hard for a long time caused Ms. Son to suffer from a herniated disc. The doctor prescribed surgery at a cost of about 60 million VND - an amount beyond the family's ability. After the surgery, her health deteriorated and she could not do heavy work as before. Every month, she still has to take maintenance medication, the expensive cost makes life even more difficult.
The house where the mother and child live was built in 2017 when their father was still alive, but because they ran out of money, only the rough parts were completed. Up to now, the house has not been painted, has not been lined with bricks, the walls are patchy, simple and lacking. That is where the four mothers and their children relied on each other to overcome difficult days.
Since their father passed away, the three Duong sisters have become more independent. Her older sister Ngo Thi Phuong Hoa (15 years old) and Duong often help their mother work in the fields on weekends. The two sisters do housework such as cooking and cleaning themselves to save their mother's trouble. The two sisters went to school on a donated bicycle, while the youngest brother, Ngo Minh Tien, had to ask his grandfather to pick him up and pick him up because the school was far away.
The family is poor, each child receives an allowance of 500,000 VND/month. This money helps mother and child cover some of their living and study expenses. Children's breakfast is often just cold rice or fasting to save money. You can only buy one set of clothes each year for Tet, the rest are old clothes given to you by relatives.
Even though he is deprived, Duong is still an innocent and affectionate boy. When asked about her dream, she just said that she wanted to redo the house for her mother, so that she could have a more complete home. As for myself, I don't expect anything more. For Duong, as long as her mother is healthy and her three sisters can continue to go to school, that is the greatest happiness.
The image of a mother in poor health but still a support for her three young children.

title: Bui Hai Dang
short_description: Bui Hai Dang, 13 years old, is currently a student at Duong Lam Secondary School (Son Tay ward, Hanoi city). Dang's life consists of only mother and child relying on each other in a small, degraded house, after his father passed away in 2018 from cirrhosis of the liver.
story:
Bui Hai Dang, 13 years old, is currently a student at Duong Lam Secondary School (Son Tay ward, Hanoi city). Dang's life consists of only mother and child relying on each other in a small, degraded house, after his father passed away in 2018 from cirrhosis of the liver.
Both Dang and his mother, Phan Thi Yen (42 years old), have congenital heart disease. When she was healthier, Ms. Yen used to work as a construction worker to make a living. But in recent years, her health has deteriorated, so she can only work at a small cake making facility near her home. Without a car, she had to walk to work. The job is unstable, can only work 2-3 times a week, only during the festival season is there a regular job. Every day they earn about 150,000 VND but their income is unstable, causing both mother and daughter's life to always be short.
Ms. Yen's heart disease requires regular check-ups every 2-3 months, but because she doesn't have enough money, she will only go to the doctor when she can collect enough money. Each month, medicine costs about 1,200,000 VND. She often has back pain, dizziness, lightheadedness, and difficulty walking. There were times when there was no money left in the family to buy food, so mother and child had to go to their grandmother's house to ask for food to make it through the meal.
Dang also has the same heart disease as his mother, and was diagnosed with pulmonary valve regurgitation. I often have chest tightness, difficulty breathing and am exempted from physical education at school. Due to lack of treatment conditions, I was not allowed to take medicine separately but had to share it with my mother. Mom takes 2/3 of a pill and saves the rest for Dang. The boy is skinny and pale, but tries to cycle to school every day despite his poor health.
Knowing my situation, teachers at school often provide breakfast with bread or instant noodles with a carton of milk. Dang's clothes and books were mainly donated by people. There are hardly any new clothes in the house. Dang confided, I just hope to have money to buy my mother a portion of broken rice - a dish she likes but has never eaten. I also wished I had a new car to go to school, but I never dared to say it because I was afraid my mother would make me sad.
The house the mother and child live in was built in 1994, has an old tile roof, and leaks everywhere. Every time it rains, there is almost no dry space left in the house, so mother and child have to line it with foam sheets and hug each other to wait for the rain to stop. The house doesn't have a stove, because it was too difficult in the past, so my mother sold the only gas stove. Currently, mother and daughter cook using an old electric stove given to them by relatives. The house also does not have a bathroom, the toilet is only a temporary open-air structure. Every day, Dang has to mix warm water and carry it to a small corner in the kitchen for his mother to bathe because she cannot carry heavy loads.
Despite illness and deprivation, Dang is still an affectionate boy who always loves his mother and tries to study. For me, the biggest dream is not for myself, but just for my mother to be healthier so that we can live peacefully together.

title: Nguyen Phuong Thao
short_description: Nguyen Phuong Thao, 13 years old, 8th grade student at Phu Lang Secondary School - Phu Lang commune, Bac Ninh province. At an age where she still had many dreams, Thao had to face a huge incident - the sudden passing of her father after a drowning accident on the way home.
story:
Nguyen Phuong Thao, 13 years old, 8th grade student at Phu Lang Secondary School - Phu Lang commune, Bac Ninh province. At an age where she still had many dreams, Thao had to face a huge incident - the sudden passing of her father after a drowning accident on the way home.
Previously, my parents were both workers, working together, saving every penny to raise my three sisters. Although the family is not wealthy, it is always full of laughter. However, the incident happened so suddenly that all three sisters still cannot calm down. Every time her father is mentioned, Thao's eyes drop and become silent because she remembers.
Since her husband's death, her mother - Ms. La Thi Thanh (43 years old) has become the sole breadwinner of the family. With a salary of about 5 million VND/month, she has to shoulder all living expenses and raise three small children when she has overtime. When working days last late into the night or overnight, she has to send her children to sleep at her grandmother's house nearby so she can go to work with peace of mind.
The lives of the three sisters are therefore also associated with early independence. The children know how to take care of each other and give in to each other so that their mothers can feel secure. In addition to working at the company, Thao's mother also took advantage of farming on the land her grandparents lent her, but the harvest was only enough to eat for a few months.
The house where the mother and her children live was built in 1999 and is now seriously degraded. The walls are peeling, the ceiling is moldy, and many leaks have to be temporarily covered with plastic every time it rains. In that old house, love is still the only thing that is always enough.
After school, Thao often observes her mother's thin, tired figure after long shifts. At those times, I just wished I could do something bigger. My simple dream is that one day I can buy my mother a massage chair, so that she can relieve pain after months of hard work.
Thao is an emotional and strong-willed girl. I love Chinese, I study it online with the hope of doing a job related to this language in the future. But most of all, what I'm most worried about is whether my mother will have enough strength to continue sending all three sisters to school.
For Thao, studying is not only a dream, but also the only way to change the future, so that her family can get through these difficult days. I hope that my two younger siblings and I will have a good education, so that one day we can become a strong support for our mother - the person who silently carries the whole world on her shoulders.
Amidst the losses and worries, Thao still maintains a very beautiful belief: as long as she continues to learn and try, the future will definitely be bright.

title: Nguyen Dieu Linh
short_description: Nguyen Dieu Linh, 13 years old, is currently a student at An Hiep Primary and Secondary School, A Sao commune, Hung Yen province. Linh's childhood did not have her father's arms, and she never grew up under the full care of her mother. But amidst these deprivations, she was still raised by the steadfast love of her grandparents.
story:
Nguyen Dieu Linh, 13 years old, is currently a student at An Hiep Primary and Secondary School, A Sao commune, Hung Yen province. Linh's childhood did not have her father's arms, and she never grew up under the full care of her mother. But amidst these deprivations, she was still raised by the steadfast love of her grandparents.
Linh's mother - Ms. Nguyen Thi Kim Thuy suffered from severe mental illness due to the effects of Agent Orange. After giving birth to Linh, her illness became worse, she often had seizures and had uncontrollable symptoms. Her family was forced to take her for long-term treatment at the hospital. Since then, Linh has only been able to see her mother for a few rare days each Tet, and then has to be away from her for another long year.
Even though she has never been hugged or cared for by her mother like other children, Linh still has a very deep affection for her mother. Every time I talk about my mother, I can't hold back my tears. What I crave the most is simply to be hugged and loved by my mother once - something that seems very normal, but is a luxury for me.
The people who have raised Linh for her mother over the years are her grandparents. His maternal grandfather, Mr. Nguyen Ngoc Anh (77 years old), is a veteran, currently living on an allowance of 3,600,000 VND per month. It is also the main source of income for the whole family. After a traffic accident in 2024, his health deteriorated significantly and he could only do light work around the house.
Grandmother, Mrs. Doan Thi Can (75 years old), is in poor health, had brain tumor surgery, now has reduced vision and often has headaches. Even so, she still tries to manage the housework, occasionally working in the fields to have rice to eat. All heavy work such as plowing and harvesting must be done by hiring people.
Their house was built in 1999 and is now seriously degraded, with many dilapidated areas. The family still owes their neighbors about 20 million VND - a large amount of money that they still cannot pay.
Understanding the situation, Linh always lives very docile and independent. I have never complained, although sometimes I can't help but feel sorry for myself when I see my friends being picked up and taken care of by their parents. After school, I help my grandparents with housework, from washing dishes, sweeping the yard to drying clothes, trying to share some of the burden of their old age.
In studying, Linh always strives and achieves good results for many years in a row. For me, studying is not only a future path, but also a way to not disappoint those who have sacrificed their lives for me.
Amid the disadvantages, Linh's dream is not something big. I just hope my grandparents are always healthy, so they can continue to stay by my side, protect and accompany me. And deep in that little heart, there is always a simple wish: one day, to fully feel a mother's love - even if it's just a hug.

title: Bui Minh Ngoc
short_description: Bui Minh Ngoc, 14 years old, is currently a student at Thai Hoc Secondary School - Chu Van An Ward, City. Hai Phong. Ngoc's childhood was not just about going to class, but also about a journey with her family to overcome illness and many deprivations.
story:
Bui Minh Ngoc, 14 years old, is currently a student at Thai Hoc Secondary School - Chu Van An Ward, City. Hai Phong. Ngoc's childhood was not just about going to class, but also about a journey with her family to overcome illness and many deprivations.
Ngoc lives with her grandmother - Mrs. Le Thi Be (64 years old), mother Pham Thi Hoa (40 years old) and two younger siblings. Two years ago, her mother was diagnosed with syringomyelia with myeloma, causing half of her body to be paralyzed and her limbs to twitch. Even though he had undergone surgery, the doctor concluded that further intervention was no longer possible. From then on, Ms. Hoa could only maintain medication and regular physical therapy to cope with her lifelong illness.
After the incident, Ngoc and her mother had to return to live at their grandmother's house. Not long after that, my father left with the excuse of working far away and since then has never returned, nor has he once provided for his children. That small family now only has to rely on each other during these challenging days.
Each month, the cost of medicine for my mother alone is about 1 million VND - not a small expense. Meanwhile, the entire family's living expenses depend on her grandmother. Mrs. Be makes a living from a small cafe, earning only 20,000 - 50,000 VND per day. In addition, she also makes votive papers, but the whole year she only earns about 3 million VND. Every penny of income must be saved to take care of meals, medicine and the family's life.
Understanding the situation, Ngoc always tries her best to study and actively participate in school activities, hoping to receive small rewards to help her family. After each class, I rush home to help the sales lady, take care of my mother, and look after my siblings.
Ngoc's two younger siblings are also very understanding. The children are always by their mother's side, massaging and helping her with daily activities. It is the love and attachment of the three sisters that is the greatest source of encouragement for Ms. Hoa to continue fighting her illness.
Every time she sees her mother in pain due to a relapse, Ngoc cannot hide her worry. I regularly massage my mother, just hoping her pain will be somewhat less. But what worries me the most is the fact that my mother cannot maintain regular physical therapy sessions due to lack of funds, causing her health to deteriorate.
Ngoc never blamed her father. But every time I mention it, I stay silent, tears fall from self-pity. In that little girl's heart, the dream was not something big - simply for the family to be at peace, for her mother to be less sick, and for everyone to be healthy and be together.
Amidst the mounting difficulties, Ngoc still grew up resiliently, carrying with her a deep love for family and an admirable determination - like a small but persistent bright spot in challenging days.

title: Tran Thi Thu Hue
short_description: Tran Thi Thu Hue, 13 years old, is currently a student at Duy Nhat Secondary School - Vu Tien commune, Hung Yen province. At an age where there are still many dreams, Hue soon gets used to a life of deprivation and worries that do not belong to childhood.
story:
Tran Thi Thu Hue, 13 years old, is currently a student at Duy Nhat Secondary School - Vu Tien commune, Hung Yen province. At an age where there are still many dreams, Hue soon gets used to a life of deprivation and worries that do not belong to childhood.
Hue lives with her father, Tran Van Thao (54 years old) and older brother Tran Duc Tuyen (17 years old). Both of my only relatives have mental health problems. Hue's father has been mentally disabled since childhood, and his ability to care for himself and handle daily activities is very limited. Not only that, after a stroke, his health became weaker with many diseases such as arthritis, hearing loss... walking also became difficult. Hue's older brother is also cognitively delayed, absorbs more slowly than his peers, and is currently only in 8th grade.
Her mother died of cancer when Hue was only 2 years old. The memory of her mother is almost gone, leaving only a silent void in her heart. From then on, father and son relied on each other in extremely difficult circumstances, when no one was capable of becoming the family's breadwinner.
The family's main source of income is only 1,000,000 VND in father's monthly allowance. After paying for electricity and water, the little money left is for daily meals. Because my dad doesn't know how to cook, our meals only revolve around boiled vegetables and occasionally a little minced meat. Prolonged deprivation left all three weak and in poor health.
During those difficult days, Hue's family was fortunate to have an eldest uncle - Mr. Tran Van Bao, who always silently helped. Not only did he provide daily food support, he also lent 60 million VND for Hue's father to have hip surgery, helping him to be able to walk like now. On weekends, the two Hue brothers often go to their uncle's house to help with work, as a simple way to express their gratitude.
After school, Hue returned to the small house and worked with her brother to cook, wash dishes, clean... instead of doing the work that adults should have done. Even though she was young, she became a spiritual support for the whole family.
Hue is a shy girl, quiet, and her health is not good. But I always have very beautiful dreams. I love cooking and hope to become a chef in the future, to cook many delicious dishes for my father and brother, so that the family can have more filling meals. I also wish I could make money, take care of my loved ones and repay Uncle Bao.
In addition, Hue also loves drawing. When I'm sad, I turn to paper and colors as a way to hide my feelings. My small dream is just to have a new color box, because the old one is almost broken, there is no longer enough for me to continue painting my dreams.
In the midst of many deprivations in life, Hue still grew up quietly, carrying in her heart a simple wish: to be educated, to be loved, and one day, to be able to take care of her small family herself.

title: Ninh Thi Yen Trang
short_description: Ninh Thi Yen Trang, 11 years old, is currently a student at Dan Hoi Secondary School, Cam Ly commune, Bac Ninh province. At an age too young to fully understand life's losses, Trang had to witness and experience pain that is not easy to name.
story:
Ninh Thi Yen Trang, 11 years old, is currently a student at Dan Hoi Secondary School, Cam Ly commune, Bac Ninh province. At an age too young to fully understand life's losses, Trang had to witness and experience pain that is not easy to name.
In September 2025, my father - the family's sole breadwinner - suddenly passed away from a stroke after a day at work. That unexpected departure not only left a large void in the small house, but also became a haunting memory when it was Trang who discovered her father. From then on, whenever I missed my father, I could only sit quietly in front of the altar, look at his photo and whisper stories that had not yet been told.
Trang's mother left the two sisters when the younger sister was very young. Many years passed, my mother did not come back, nor did she once ask or convey anything. From then on, the three grandchildren - grandmother, Trang and younger sister Yen Nhi became each other's only dependents.
My grandmother, Mrs. Vu Thi Tich (69 years old), is elderly and in poor health, no longer able to work. Previously, my grandfather had a stroke for 12 years and passed away in 2022. Successive events made the already difficult family even more unstable. Currently, the lives of the three grandchildren mainly rely on the support of neighbors and the community.
The house the family lives in was built from selling a piece of land, but has only been completed. There are almost no valuable assets inside, and all appliances are old. Even the money for painting the house was still owed, but the store owner quietly did not ask for it, because he clearly understood the family's situation.
Every day, Trang rides her bike to school. Yen Nhi's younger sister goes to school by bus, which costs 500,000 VND/month - an amount that her grandmother can no longer afford. The family had to ask for debt from the school, hoping to be able to manage it by the end of the year.
Life is lacking, but human love is clearly present. Neighbors often bring rice, food, and clothes to help. Those shares became a fulcrum for the three of them to continue getting through each day.
Trang's younger sister shows signs of hyperactivity, decreased attention, and often loses focus in studying. But because the situation was too difficult, my family could not take me to see a doctor. Trang is both an older sister and a little mother, always caring for and taking care of her younger siblings to the best of her ability.
Despite many injuries, Yen Trang still harbors a beautiful dream: to become a doctor. I want to take care of my grandmother, my younger sister, and most of all, help people at risk of illness like my father, so that no one has to pass away suddenly, leaving behind heartbreaking gaps like my family experienced.
Amidst the loss and deprivation, that 11-year-old girl still grew up quietly, carrying in her heart a simple but humane dream - the dream of healing, not only for others, but also for the unhealed wounds in her life.

title: Nguyen Hai Yen
short_description: Nguyen Hai Yen, 11 years old, is currently a student at Vinh Hoa Secondary School, Ninh Giang commune, city. Hai Phong. At an age when she should only know carefree school days and innocent laughter, Yen soon grew up amid the losses and worries of adults.
story:
Nguyen Hai Yen, 11 years old, is currently a student at Vinh Hoa Secondary School, Ninh Giang commune, city. Hai Phong. At an age when she should only know carefree school days and innocent laughter, Yen soon grew up amid the losses and worries of adults.
In 2023, my father passed away from a heart attack, ending his unfinished promise to return for Tet, buy new clothes for his children and repair the old roof. From then on, in the small house, the father's void remained, quietly and tormented. Two years have passed, but for Yen, the memory of her father has never gone away.
My mother - Ms. Trinh Thi Thuy, 36 years old, became the sole breadwinner of the family. With no support left, she took her two children home to live on her grandmother's land. The house was temporarily built and had not yet been completed, but it was degraded. Every rainy season it became even more difficult when water leaked everywhere, and could only provide temporary cover for the day.
To conveniently take care of her child, Ms. Thuy applied to work as a garment worker near her home. Every day, she rushes home from early morning, rushes home to cook rice for her children, and then returns to the workshop. Unable to work overtime, her income is only about 5 million VND/month, after deducting living expenses, electricity and water... there is almost nothing left. Her lack of life has forced her to borrow often, paying back as much as she can.
Yen's grandmother - Mrs. Vu Thi Thanh, 55 years old, despite her poor health, still tries to work to help her daughter take care of her grandchildren. Her silent sacrifice made Ms. Thuy sometimes unable to hide her torment.
Every day, Yen goes to school on the old bicycle that has followed her since 3rd grade, but now the saddle is torn and the frame is rusted. I used to hope to have a new car to take my younger brother to school, but I never dared to tell my mother, because I understood how hard it was for her.
Yen's younger brother, Nguyen Quang Minh, is still young but very understanding. I always said that I would try to grow up quickly to become a support for my mother and sister. Whenever they saw their mother silently shedding tears, the two sisters could only run to hug her tightly, as the only way to share their sadness.
Yen is a girl rich in feelings and thoughts beyond her years. If I had some money, the first thing I want to do is not buy anything for myself, but buy a gift for my mother - the woman who has never thought about herself for many years.
My dream is not big, just simple but full of love: one day I can build a new house for my mother, where there will be no more worries about rain leaks, no more anxious nights due to wind and storms... so that my parents and I can live more peaceful days after all the losses we have experienced.

title: Nguyen Thi Huyen
short_description: Nguyen Thi Huyen, 14 years old, is currently a student at Quynh Khe Secondary and Secondary School, living with her mother in Nguyen Du commune, Hung Yen province. Huyen doesn't have a father and grew up in the arms of her mother - Nguyen Thi Tuyen, 58 years old. For more than ten years, mother and daughter relied on each other in the old house left by their grandparents, quietly going through the difficult days.
story:
Nguyen Thi Huyen, 14 years old, is currently a student at Quynh Khe Secondary and Secondary School, living with her mother in Nguyen Du commune, Hung Yen province. Huyen doesn't have a father and grew up in the arms of her mother - Nguyen Thi Tuyen, 58 years old. For more than ten years, mother and daughter relied on each other in the old house left by their grandparents, quietly going through the difficult days.
Huyen's mother is suffering from many illnesses. She has shingles on her face, the lesions spread quickly, affecting her vision and is currently being treated for conjunctivitis at the hospital. Without relatives to take care of her, during the days her mother was in the hospital, Huyen had to stay home alone and take care of everything herself. At the end of the week, I cycled more than 30 minutes to the hospital to visit my mother, just hoping to see her a little to ease my worries.
The mother and child's livelihood depends only on the 4 acres of fields left by their grandparents. There are two crops per year, after deducting expenses and keeping rice, each crop only costs about 1 million VND. Unable to work far away due to poor health, Huyen's mother took up knitting curtains at home, earning 9,000 VND each. Even though she works from morning until night, she can only complete 4-5 pieces a day. Life is therefore always difficult, lacking before and after. There are times when the owner of the facility has to advance money so that mother and child can survive the day.
During the most difficult days, mother and child lived thanks to the support of neighbors and an uncle. When her mother was in the hospital, people in the neighborhood called Huyen over to eat, or brought food to her house. The house the mother and child are living in was built more than 20 years ago. Even though the roof was supported in 2020, it still leaks in many places. Every time it rains heavily, water floods the bed and the mother and child have to move to the middle of the house to sleep temporarily.
In front of their yard, mother and daughter grew more vegetables to improve their meals. The house is located deep in the village, surrounded by fields, the roads are deserted, especially in the evening they are even more lonely. Huyen goes to school on an old bicycle donated by the locality, which has broken down many times. I just hope to have an electric bike in the future to make studying less difficult when I get to high school.
Huyen is a bright and active girl, but deep inside she always carries self-pity. Every time my father was mentioned, I just remained silent, not daring to ask my mother because I was afraid she would be upset. I told myself that just having my mother by my side was enough, because since birth, my mother had shouldered both the roles of both parents alone to raise me.
There was a time when, because it was too difficult, Huyen's mother thought about letting her drop out of school. But Huyen persisted in persuading her mother, hoping to continue going to school. I cherish the dream of becoming a singer, actively participating in cultural activities to nurture my passion.
In the midst of both material and emotional deprivation, Huyen is always optimistic and strong. What I hope most is not for myself, but just to have money to buy some new clothes for my mother, because my mother is used to wearing other people's old clothes, giving all the best for her children.
A small wish, but containing all the love and gratitude of a little girl who soon understood what sacrifice was.

title: Nguyen Thi Thao
short_description: Nguyen Thi Thao, 17 years old, is currently studying at the College of Tourism and Industry and Trade, Hai Phong city. I live with my mother and two children in Tue Tinh commune. That small family just went through a big incident when my father passed away in January 2025 after a long battle with liver disease.
story:
Nguyen Thi Thao, 17 years old, is currently studying at the College of Tourism and Industry and Trade, Hai Phong city. I live with my mother and two children in Tue Tinh commune. That small family just went through a big incident when my father passed away in January 2025 after a long battle with liver disease.
Before that, my father had liver stones, underwent many surgeries, his health deteriorated and he completely lost the ability to work. His father's passing not only left a spiritual loss, but also caused the economic burden to fall on the thin shoulders of his mother - Ms. Hoang Thi Vinh, 47 years old.
Currently, Ms. Vinh is the family's sole breadwinner. She works as a tailor at home, every day she has to work from 5 am to 11 pm to earn about 100,000 - 150,000 VND. Previously, she worked at a factory, but since her husband passed away, she asked to bring the machine home to work and take care of her children. However, with a small amount of work and income, the lives of the four mothers and their children are always in a state of scarcity.
Ms. Vinh herself also has many diseases. She had surgery for a thyroid tumor and still has to take maintenance medication to avoid recurrence. Her body is small and her health is weak, but she still tries hard every day for her children. Every time she mentions the current situation, she cannot hide her tears because of helplessness.
Thao's younger brother is Nguyen Phu An, 14 years old, with many complex medical conditions. I have short-limbed dwarfism syndrome, which prevents my height from growing normally. In addition, An also has inguinal degeneration, open knee joints, and is at risk of paralysis of both legs if he does not control his weight. I also have mild hydrocephalus. On cold days, my body aches and it becomes more difficult to walk. However, every day An still tries to walk about 15 minutes to school. For me, just being able to study with friends is a great happiness.
The youngest sister, Nguyen Thi Khanh Ngoc, 9 years old, also had the disease since childhood. She has epilepsy and is severely malnourished and stunted, weighing less than 15kg. Every month, my family has to take me to Hanoi for a follow-up check-up, which costs about 2-3 million VND for medicine alone. Although her body is as small as a 5-year-old child, Ngoc is very alert and understanding.
In that situation, Thao soon became a spiritual support for the whole family. After school, I help my mother with sewing. There are days when we work until late at night before we dare to take a break. Every summer, Thao proactively interns at companies near her home to accumulate experience, with the hope of soon having a stable job to help her mother.
The family has been poor for many years, and each child receives an orphan allowance of 500,000 VND/month. The house where the mother and child live was built in 1975 and is now seriously degraded, damp and dilapidated.
Even though life is still too difficult, Thao and her three sisters have never complained. The children clearly understand their mother's hardships, so they always try to be obedient, hardworking, and do everything within their ability.
Your dream is not something far-fetched. Just having a stable job, earning enough money to take care of their mother and help their children continue treatment and go to school, for them, that is a full future.

title: Pham Nguyen Chien
short_description: Pham Nguyen Chien, 13 years old, is currently a student at Mong Duong Secondary School, living with his grandparents in Mong Duong ward, Quang Ninh province. I don't have a father, and my mother left when Chien was only 1 month and 20 days old. Since then, my mother has completely lost contact, leaving an irreplaceable void in my life and the silent pain of my grandparents.
story:
Pham Nguyen Chien, 13 years old, is currently a student at Mong Duong Secondary School, living with his grandparents in Mong Duong ward, Quang Ninh province. I don't have a father, and my mother left when Chien was only 1 month and 20 days old. Since then, my mother has completely lost contact, leaving an irreplaceable void in my life and the silent pain of my grandparents.
Growing up in the loving arms of his grandparents, Chien considered them as his biological parents. Grandfather Pham Nguyen Chuong, 62 years old, was a Mong Duong coal mine worker and retired 8 years ago. He currently has kidney stones and needs treatment but cannot afford surgery. Grandmother Ninh Thi Van, 62 years old, has poor health, suffers from many diseases such as diabetes, blood pressure, bone and joint problems, and often has to go to the hospital. Even though she has insurance support, every month she still has to spend more on medicine, while her family's finances are extremely struggling.
At an age when they should be resting, grandparents still work hard to raise their grandchildren. Every day, my grandmother buys broom cotton to make brooms and then takes them away to kill termites. The work is meticulous and hard, but the income is only about 3,000 VND/tree. You can make a few trees a day. It depends on the season, so it is extremely unstable. In addition, the grandparents took advantage of the land behind the house to grow more vegetables to save on living expenses.
The small house is located deep on a slope, next to the coal railway, built in 2011 and is now moldy and degraded. Inside there were only simple items, nothing valuable. That is where the three grandchildren relied on each other, quietly going through the difficult days.
Every day, Chien walks about 2 km to school. I am a quiet, timid boy. Every time I mention my grandparents, my eyes fill with tears because I feel sorry for the hardships they are enduring. After school, I help my grandmother make brooms, help them with housework, trying to share some of the burden.
Chien himself is not in good health. I often have stomach aches, and some days while studying I have to ask to go out because the pain comes. Even though she went for a check-up under health insurance, her family did not qualify for an in-depth examination, so her illness has not been clearly determined until now.
Chien's childhood lacked the love of his parents, growing up in a situation of many difficulties, illnesses and worries. But in that boy's eyes there was always a simple thing - hoping that his grandparents would have good health, and that he could study well so that in the future he could help and take care of his grandparents, who had spent the rest of their lives loving and raising him.

title: Trinh Minh Phuong
short_description: Trinh Minh Phuong, 16 years old, is currently studying at Yen Dung 3 High School, living with her mother Hoang Thi Huyen (48 years old) and older sister Trinh Hoang Linh (19 years old) in Dong Viet commune, Bac Ninh province. Phuong's childhood lacked a father figure when her father passed away from cirrhosis when she was 2 years old, leaving her mother and child to rely on each other in a small house without a breadwinner.
story:
Trinh Minh Phuong, 16 years old, is currently studying at Yen Dung 3 High School, living with her mother Hoang Thi Huyen (48 years old) and older sister Trinh Hoang Linh (19 years old) in Dong Viet commune, Bac Ninh province. Phuong's childhood lacked a father figure when her father passed away from cirrhosis when she was 2 years old, leaving her mother and child to rely on each other in a small house without a breadwinner.
Phuong's mother, Huyen, was diagnosed with liver toxicity in 2003. For many years, she has had to have her liver checked every 6 months to monitor her health. Without health insurance, each visit costs 2 to 3 million VND, becoming a huge burden for the family, which is already struggling to make ends meet.
Previously, when her eyes were still healthy, Ms. Huyen worked at a company that manufactured false eyelashes. But over time, her health deteriorated and her eyesight became increasingly poor, so she was forced to become a garment worker, with a salary of about 4.5 million VND per month. After work, she sits next to an old sewing machine to repair clothes to earn extra income. However, her job is only around the neighborhood, sometimes she only earns a few tens of thousands of dong a week, not enough to cover her living expenses.
Her family belongs to a near-poor household and is a single woman raising two children, so each month Ms. Huyen receives 1 million VND from the locality. That amount of money is just enough to pay for electricity, water and basic necessities. When in need, she has to borrow from neighbors, just hoping her children don't have to drop out of school because of difficult circumstances.
Phuong's older sister is Trinh Hoang Linh, currently a first-year student at Bac Giang Agricultural University. The dormitory fee alone is 2,050,000 VND per semester, not including other study and living expenses. With her mother's meager income, sending Linh to university is really a big burden. Because she has just entered a new environment, Linh is trying to focus on her studies, but she also plans to find a part-time job soon to take care of herself and help her mother.
Phuong is a calm, mature and very understanding girl. Seeing my mother becoming weaker and weaker due to illness and struggling to make a living, I only know how to study well so that I can help my mother in the future. When her mother was sick, Phuong quietly did everything in the house for her, from cooking, cleaning to taking care of daily life.
It is the obedience and understanding of Phuong and her sisters that are the greatest source of encouragement to help Huyen have more strength to overcome the difficult days.
Phuong's dream is very simple but meaningful. I hope one day I can enter university and become a teacher, so that I can later help other children with difficult circumstances like me continue to go to school, nurture hope and continue writing their unfinished dreams.

title: Tran Gia Huy
short_description: Tran Gia Huy, 16 years old, is currently a student at Tran Hung Dao High School, Phu Lang commune, Bac Ninh province. Huy's family has 6 people: grandmother Nguyen Thi Tinh (71 years old), father Tran Huy Lam (45 years old), mother Nguyen Thi Thinh (36 years old), and two children Tran Trung Hai (10 years old) and Tran Nguyen Dieu Linh (6 years old).
story:
Tran Gia Huy, 16 years old, is currently a student at Tran Hung Dao High School, Phu Lang commune, Bac Ninh province. Huy's family has 6 people: grandmother Nguyen Thi Tinh (71 years old), father Tran Huy Lam (45 years old), mother Nguyen Thi Thinh (36 years old), and two children Tran Trung Hai (10 years old) and Tran Nguyen Dieu Linh (6 years old).
Previously, Huy's parents were both healthy and were the family's two main workers. Although life is not abundant, it is still full of love. But a big incident happened in 2021, when Huy's father had a traffic accident on the way home from work. The serious accident left him with a severe brain injury and complete paralysis.
Since then, Mr. Lam has been living in a vegetative state, with his brain atrophied and no longer conscious. All daily activities - from eating, bathing to changing diapers - must rely entirely on the care of relatives. To maintain life, he must take blood-boosting and brain-nourishing medicine every day. In the small house, taking care of him became a familiar part of life, as the whole family took turns staying at his bedside.
Huy's grandmother is old and has diabetes, so her health is weak. She can only hang around the house cooking and feeding her son. For heavier tasks like changing diapers and cleaning Lam, she had to ask Huy and his mother to help because they no longer had the strength.
Since her husband's accident, Huy's mother became the sole breadwinner for the whole family. She works as a worker at an electronics company with a salary of about 7 million VND per month. All living expenses, food, education for the children and medicine for her husband depend on that small salary. With a family of six people, that amount of money is always in short supply.
Loving his mother's hard work, Huy goes to rent ceramic bowls every weekend. Each work session lasts up to 12 hours, I only earn about 200,000 VND, but that is all the effort of a 10th grade student who wants to share some of the burden with his mother.
There were months when the family did not have enough money to buy good medicine for his father, Huy's mother had to buy cheaper medicine to endure. The school is nearly 8 km from home, but Huy currently does not have a car to go to school. The only electric car in the house - a gift from a neighbor - was broken and no longer usable. However, Huy never complained or asked for anything.
Even though life is full of difficulties, Huy's mother always encourages her children to try their best to study. She believes that only education can help her children escape poverty and have a better future.
Understanding and loving his mother, after each class, Huy rushed back home to help his grandmother and mother take care of his father, cook, clean the house and tutor his two younger siblings in studying. That 16-year-old student is much more mature than his peers, quietly shouldering adult tasks.
Huy's biggest dreams are not far-fetched. I just hope to grow up soon, be able to make a lot of money to help my mother and buy good medicine for my father, so that he can be with the family longer.
For Huy, just having his father still present in the small house - even in silence - is a great comfort.

title: Vuong Duc Viet Anh
short_description: Vuong Duc Viet Anh, 17 years old, is currently studying at Vinh Phuc College of Economics and Technology, Vinh Phuc province. She lives with her family including grandmother Kieu Thi Doan (76 years old), mother Bui Thi Thu Huong (44 years old), twin brother Vuong Duc Phuc Anh (17 years old) and younger sister Vuong Ngoc Tram Anh (10 years old) in Phuc Tho commune, Hanoi city.
story:
Vuong Duc Viet Anh, 17 years old, is currently studying at Vinh Phuc College of Economics and Technology, Vinh Phuc province. She lives with her family including grandmother Kieu Thi Doan (76 years old), mother Bui Thi Thu Huong (44 years old), twin brother Vuong Duc Phuc Anh (17 years old) and younger sister Vuong Ngoc Tram Anh (10 years old) in Phuc Tho commune, Hanoi city.
Viet Anh's father is a doctor, previously worked and lived in Ho Chi Minh City. In 2016, he unexpectedly passed away from sudden death, leaving a huge void in the family. The shock of losing their father at such a young age caused twin brothers Viet Anh and Phuc Anh to fall into severe depression and had to undergo psychological treatment for two years. After that, the two brothers were picked up by their grandmother and taken care of. At that time, both of them had just started first grade, so their studies were slower than their peers. Although the memory of their father gradually fades over the years, the image of a once completely loving family is always imprinted in the children's minds.
The events have not stopped yet. After her husband passed away, Viet Anh's mother - Ms. Thu Huong, while working at the Tran Huu Trang Theater, was discovered to have uterine cancer. She was forced to take her three children to live with her grandmother so someone could take care of them. While being treated at the hospital, she heard that her child had an accident on the way home from school. She quickly asked to be discharged from the hospital to visit her child. At that moment, the tumor in her body ruptured, causing her to fall and hit her head on the ground, leading to a brain hematoma and stroke.
Since that incident, she often lost her memory and could not control her behavior. Because she was diagnosed with hemophilia, she could not have brain surgery right away but had to be monitored for a long time. Her eyes are almost completely blind. Every day she has to have injections to maintain her vision, costing about 300,000 VND per injection. Although the cancer has been treated, Ms. Huong still has to face many other diseases such as leaky heart valves. Each heart examination and MRI scan costs about 7 million VND, even though her family has health insurance for near-poor households. Currently, her memory has not recovered. Many times she runs away from home and wanders to the riverbank, forcing her grandmother to always watch over her day and night.
The entire family's livelihood now depends on social assistance. Grandma receives a military sick allowance of about 4,400,000 VND per month. Viet Anh's mother was recognized as disabled and received 700,000 VND per month. The three brothers each receive support of 500,000 VND per month (received quarterly). In addition, the family only raises about 10 chickens to get eggs to improve meals. The family's small house lacks everything: there is no bathroom, each time you shower you have to temporarily cover it with a wooden door in the corner of the yard; The toilet is also an open-air, temporary structure and does not ensure minimum living conditions.
Viet Anh's twin brother - Phuc Anh is currently studying in 10th grade at Minh Quang High School, Ba Vi commune, nearly 30 km from home. Understanding her situation, a parent at the school gave Phuc Anh a place to stay and sponsored all study, accommodation, and living expenses for three years of high school. Every day I was given 30,000 VND for lunch, and on weekends I took the bus to visit home.
Viet Anh is currently staying in the school's dormitory, the cost is only 100,000 VND per month and is supported by teachers at a discount. To have money to cover living expenses, I applied for a part-time job at a phone parts factory near the school, working on Saturday and Sunday nights from 5:00 p.m. to 11:00 p.m., getting paid about 300,000 VND each week. There were times when there was no money left for food, so Viet Anh had to starve until the weekend to go to work. Knowing how hard it was for her, Phuc Anh many times skipped her lunch to send her money.
The two brothers Viet Anh and Phuc Anh grew up in poverty but were very independent and understanding. Last summer, both worked as construction workers, earning about 300,000 VND per day. After saving up all summer, the two brothers bought an electric car as a means of transportation. In difficult circumstances, the two of them never asked for anything for themselves. Whatever they needed, they found ways to do extra work to make money.
Even though each person studies in a different place, the two brothers always love and support each other. Phuc Anh is quiet and introverted; Viet Anh is lively, optimistic and is often the one who encourages his brother when he is sad. During the years without the full care of their parents, the two brothers were each other's biggest spiritual support.
Phuc Anh is currently being guided by the school and nurturing her dream of studying abroad in Germany. She is trying to learn foreign languages ​​to find scholarships. Viet Anh wants to become an electronic engineer.
But above all, the two brothers' biggest wishes are not their own dreams. They just hope to have enough conditions to continue treating their mother's illness, to get a good education, so that in the future they can stand on their own and take care of their family - a small home that has gone through so much loss.

title: Nguyen Ba Phat
short_description: Nguyen Ba Phat, 16 years old, is currently a student at Dai An High School, living with his mother Nguyen Thi Tam (56 years old) and grandmother Do Thi Ngo (83 years old) in Yen Dong commune, Ninh Binh province. Since birth, Phat grew up in a family that lacked a father figure. I never asked my mother about it, as if in my heart the boy understood that there were gaps that he just needed to quietly accept.
story:
Nguyen Ba Phat, 16 years old, is currently a student at Dai An High School, living with his mother Nguyen Thi Tam (56 years old) and grandmother Do Thi Ngo (83 years old) in Yen Dong commune, Ninh Binh province. Since birth, Phat grew up in a family that lacked a father figure. I never asked my mother about it, as if in my heart the boy understood that there were gaps that he just needed to quietly accept.
Phat's mother, Ms. Tam, has had poor health since childhood and is currently receiving support for victims affected by Agent Orange, with a subsidy of 2 million VND per month. She has many underlying diseases, her body is often tired, and she easily has difficulty breathing when overworked. However, to have more income to raise her children and take care of her elderly mother, she still tries to do sewing work at home. Thanks to receiving free vocational training support, she can sew hats for garment factories. The salary is only 600 VND per car, so even though she works from early morning to late at night, she only earns about 60,000 VND per day.
The family still has about 1 acres of land left by their grandparents. Because her health does not allow, Ms. Tam cannot directly farm, sometimes she has to hire someone to help her. Each harvest yields only 2-3 bags of rice, mainly for storing food at home. In addition, she raises 4 more chickens to get eggs to improve her daily meals. Knowing the difficult situation, mother and daughter always spend very sparingly, every penny is carefully calculated.
Previously, the family also included Phat's grandfather. But in 2020, he died of stomach cancer, leaving behind mother and child and an elderly grandmother. Mrs. Ngo is now 83 years old, blind and bedridden, and has Alzheimer's syndrome so she often loses consciousness and speaks uncontrollably. All of her daily activities depend entirely on the care of Phat and her mother.
The house where the three people live was built more than 20 years ago and is now seriously degraded. The tile roof and brick floor are old and leaky in many places. Every time it rains heavily and water leaks into the house, mother and child have to stretch a tarpaulin on the ceiling to catch the water. However, the small house is always kept neat and clean. The most valuable asset in the house is her grandfather's altar, which Ms. Tam bought with all the money paid for his funeral after his death. The family also has a TV given to them by their uncle, but it is now broken and cannot be repaired.
Phat is also somewhat influenced by his mother's health. Whenever I'm stressed, I often have headaches and difficulty concentrating. However, growing up in circumstances lacking both material resources and family health, Phat is still an optimistic student, always keeping a gentle smile on his face. I am very attached to my mother and often confide in her. Knowing that his mother gets tired easily and has difficulty breathing, Phat never dares to disobey or make her sad.
After school, I help my mother sew hats to earn extra income. Phat's school bike was given to him in 6th grade. It is now old and broken many times, but he still uses it every day. Phat never dared to dream of a new car. For me, just having the means to go to school is a blessing.
What Phat desires most is not for himself. I just wish my mother, grandmother and myself could live peacefully together. Whenever mentioning her son, Ms. Tam always speaks with a proud voice. For her, Phat is the most valuable asset, the greatest gift that life has given her.
Phat especially loves History and studies this subject very well. I am currently a member of the school's excellent student team. Phat's biggest dream is to pass the entrance exam to a university in Hanoi, find a stable job so that he can take care of his mother and grandmother in the future, making up for the hard years his small family has gone through.

title: Le Ha Huyen Trang
short_description: Le Ha Huyen Trang, 16 years old, is currently a student at the Yen Khanh Vocational and Continuing Education Center. Trang is living with her 79-year-old grandmother To Thi Hue in Khanh Hoi commune, Ninh Binh province.
story:
Le Ha Huyen Trang, 16 years old, is currently a student at the Yen Khanh Vocational and Continuing Education Center. Trang is living with her 79-year-old grandmother To Thi Hue in Khanh Hoi commune, Ninh Binh province.
Trang grew up in an incomplete family as her parents divorced when she was very young. After that breakup, my father no longer raised or provided any money for mother and child.
For many years, Trang's mother, Ha Thi Van, worked as a garment worker to take care of her daughter's education. Because of her poor health, she had to rent a room near the company to make it easier to get to work, and only came home to visit her children once a week. Trang's income of about 5 million VND per month is just enough to cover living and study expenses. Even though life is hard, for Trang, as long as her mother is by her side, all difficulties seem to be overcome.
But the biggest event in my life suddenly happened on December 2, 2025. While on the way home from work, Trang's mother had a traffic accident. The fall caused her serious injuries and she did not survive.
The sudden passing of her mother was a huge shock for Trang. The mother I love the most, and the only spiritual support in my life, has left forever.
From then on, the small house was left with only the two of them relying on each other. The situation is already difficult, but now they have lost their main worker, making life even more difficult. Trang's family has been a poor household in the locality for many years. The house, built in 1990, is now seriously degraded: the old tile roof is leaking, the walls are damp and peeling in many places. On days when it rains heavily, Trang has to stretch plastic in her bedroom to avoid rainwater from splashing in.
My grandmother is nearly 80 years old, has poor health and suffers from high blood pressure, and is no longer able to work. She can only cook simple meals for her two grandchildren. All big and small things in the family today mainly rely on help from Trang's aunts. My mother has four sisters, but they are all freelance workers with unstable income, so they cannot provide much support.
Trang herself also has many health problems. Since 4th grade, I often had difficulty breathing and chest tightness. The doctor diagnosed me with heart problems and had to take medication for many years. In addition, I also have dermatitis, seborrhea on my legs and persistent stomach pain. Because of her poor health, Trang was exempted from studying Physical Education by the school.
However, the little girl still harbors a simple dream in her heart: to become a veterinarian, because she loves animals very much. But given the current situation, Trang's path ahead is still unclear, making that dream seem increasingly distant.
Trang misses her mother very much. Even though my mother has to work far away and cannot be with me often, in Trang's heart, she is always the person who loves me the most in the world. When my mother passed away, I felt like I lost the biggest spiritual support in life.
Although sad and disappointed, Trang still tried to get up, continue to go to school and study hard every day. Because in my heart, there is always a simple desire: to live well so that my mother far away can feel secure and proud of her.

title: Pham Hai Yen
short_description: Pham Hai Yen, 13 years old, is currently a student at Cam Ly Secondary School, living in Cam Ly commune, Bac Ninh province. Yen's family has 6 people including grandmother, father Pham Dinh Nghia (41 years old), mother Trinh Thi Hang (37 years old), Yen and two younger brothers Pham Phu Binh (in grade 3) and Pham Binh An (in grade 2).
story:
Pham Hai Yen, 13 years old, is currently a student at Cam Ly Secondary School, living in Cam Ly commune, Bac Ninh province. Yen's family has 6 people including grandmother, father Pham Dinh Nghia (41 years old), mother Trinh Thi Hang (37 years old), Yen and two younger brothers Pham Phu Binh (in grade 3) and Pham Binh An (in grade 2).
The family's life was once peaceful like any other home, until an unexpected event occurred. While returning home from work, Mr. Nghia had a serious traffic accident. The fall left him with a serious brain injury, requiring prolonged treatment from September 2024 until February 2025 before being discharged from the hospital. But after all the efforts to cure him, he was no longer as alert as before. Currently, he has completely lost consciousness, lives in a vegetative state, is completely paralyzed, and depends on relatives to take care of him for all activities.
From a family with a healthy husband who was the economic breadwinner, Ms. Hang suddenly had to take care of the entire family of six people alone. Currently, she works as a construction assistant, the work is hard and completely dependent on the weather. Each month she can only work about 10-15 working days, at most no more than 20 days, with an income of 250,000 VND/day. To treat her husband for a long time, the family had to exhaust all the money they had accumulated and even borrow more.
Not only does she work hard at the construction site, Ms. Hang also has to take care of her husband's every meal and sleep. Every morning she goes to work early, at lunch break she rushes home to take care of her husband and then goes back to work. Austere wrinkles and fatigue were clearly visible on that woman's face. She herself is also sick and has to spend 2-3 million VND on medicine every month, but she still tries to endure it to save money for her family.
Yen's grandmother is old, has heart disease, and has to take medicine every day. Her poor health prevented her from working, so she could only stay home to take care of her son so that her daughter-in-law could go to work with peace of mind.
Currently, the family is considered poor in the locality. Mr. Nghia receives a disability allowance of 1,300,000 VND/month, but this amount is only enough to buy maintenance medicine, diapers and personal hygiene items for him, making it almost impossible to share additional living expenses.
Every day, Hai Yen goes to school on an old bicycle. The youngest child, Binh An, has to go to school by school bus at a cost of 200,000 VND/month because his mother does not have time to pick him up. Since her father's illness, Yen has become much more mature and understanding. After school, I help my mother with housework, look after my younger siblings and help take care of my father so that my mother can go to work with peace of mind.
Whenever mentioning family, Hai Yen's eyes often droop, filled with a deep sadness. I remember the days when my family was still full, when my father was still healthy and the whole family gathered together. I hope everything can go back to the way it was before, even though I understand in my heart that it's far away.
What that 13-year-old girl wants most right now is not big dreams for herself. I just hope that my mother will have less hardship, less burden on her thin shoulders, so that my three sisters can grow up in peaceful days with her.
